title: Headscale, Cloudflare, and the Great Administrative Vanishing Act
theme: Private mesh for admin access, public ingress left to the grown-ups
style_anchor: clean technical storyboard with restrained cinematic framing and dry systems humor

protagonist.description: A pragmatic infrastructure operator in a dark hoodie, carrying a laptop and a coffee that has clearly outlived its optimism
protagonist.silhouette: lean figure, laptop under one arm, hoodie, slightly hunched posture, focused gaze

scenes.prompt:
Human-Readable house style: a mostly monochrome, hand-drawn editorial webcomic. The recurring protagonist is a minimalist stick figure with a round head, dot eyes, and a consistent silhouette. Backgrounds are detailed and grounded; characters remain deliberately simple. Use clean black ink, restrained hatching, and at most one warm orange accent color. Dialogue is short, legible, and hand-lettered in plain speech bubbles. The humor is dry, observational, technically aware, and never explained.

clean technical storyboard with restrained cinematic framing and dry systems humor

Render one self-contained cinematic comic scene, not a multi-panel page. Camera: wide.

Location: city rooftop overlooking a small server room skylight. Scene: A split-screen style rooftop view: one side shows the public site bsbikebites.com on a browser, the other a private admin stack labeled Headscale. The visual joke is that they are adjacent only in the architectural diagram, not in the trust boundary.

Recurring protagonist (protagonist_claude): A pragmatic infrastructure operator in a dark hoodie, carrying a laptop and a coffee that has clearly outlived its optimism Silhouette: lean figure, laptop under one arm, hoodie, slightly hunched posture, focused gaze

Keep the protagonist's proportions, face, and silhouette identical across every scene. Accent color: wireguard_blue.

Visible prop continuity:
- prop_split_diagram_board: diagram; A whiteboard with two overlapping circles labeled public ingress and private mesh, with a neat note that the overlap is only conceptual; introduces the network model for the whole sequence
- prop_laptop_bsbikebites_browser: site tab; A laptop open to bsbikebites.com showing a public storefront page with a cheerful bike-bite logo; public-facing website remains visible as the non-admin surface

World prop reference (IDs are metadata; never print an ID in the artwork):
prop_split_diagram_board: diagram; A whiteboard with two overlapping circles labeled public ingress and private mesh, with a neat note that the overlap is only conceptual; continuity: introduces the network model for the whole sequence
prop_laptop_bsbikebites_browser: site tab; A laptop open to bsbikebites.com showing a public storefront page with a cheerful bike-bite logo; continuity: public-facing website remains visible as the non-admin surface
prop_headscale_compose_stack: compose file; A terminal window showing a docker compose file with Headscale added as one more service; continuity: Headscale is introduced as the admin-plane control service
prop_status_led_green: status LED; A tiny green LED on the rack that blinks with almost suspicious confidence; continuity: background continuity marker for the server room scenes
prop_headscale_user_creation_terminal: user creation command; A terminal prompt displaying headscale users create bsbb followed by preauth key generation; continuity: shows the exact administrative bootstrap step
prop_notebook_key_list: notebook; A spiral notebook with a hand-drawn list of devices and a neat box around the new user name bsbb; continuity: reinforces the user identity and the admin rollout
prop_tailscale_up_terminal: tailscale up command; A terminal showing tailscale up --login-server https://your-headscale:8080 --authkey ...; continuity: connects enrolled devices to the mesh
prop_phone_mesh_badge: phone; A smartphone displaying a mesh-connected badge and the same tailnet name as the laptop; continuity: one of the enrolled admin devices
prop_laptop_mesh_badge: laptop badge; The protagonist’s laptop now shows a small mesh icon and a 100.64.x.x address tag; continuity: one of the enrolled admin devices
prop_ssh_terminal_mesh: ssh session; A terminal connected over the tailnet to a remote host at 100.64.0.2; continuity: demonstrates admin access over the private mesh
prop_deleted_hostname_sticker: hostname sticker; A sticker on the monitor bezel where ssh.bsbikebites.com used to be, now crossed out in black marker; continuity: marks the removed public SSH entry point
prop_admin_surface_diagram: admin path diagram; A whiteboard diagram showing SSH, Postgres, and Proxmox UI moving behind the Headscale-managed mesh; continuity: communicates the replacement of Cloudflare on the admin plane
prop_blue_marker: blue marker; A blue dry-erase marker with a slightly chewed cap; continuity: used to draw the mesh path throughout the storyboard
prop_proxmox_login_page: proxmox ui; A browser tab showing the Proxmox login page reachable only through the tailnet; continuity: one of the admin services now behind the mesh
prop_postgres_connection_string: database string; A terminal with a Postgres connection string routed to the mesh address rather than the public internet; continuity: one of the admin services now behind the mesh
prop_public_ingress_sign: ingress sign; A small sign near the front desk that says public ingress remains separate from the admin mesh; continuity: reinforces that public visitors are not tailnet members
prop_bike_bites_poster: poster; A colorful poster for bsbikebites.com with a bike and snack motif, still happily public; continuity: keeps the customer-facing brand visible and untouched by admin changes
prop_tailnet_health_panel: health panel; A dashboard showing connected devices, stable 100.64.x.x addresses, and no public SSH exposure; continuity: confirms the completed admin-plane migration
prop_cooling_fan: fan; A slow-spinning cooling fan on the desk that makes the room feel more operational than emotional; continuity: ambient closing detail for the final scene

Dialogue to reproduce exactly and legibly:
- “Overlap is not the same as replacement.”
- “The internet remains uninvited, as usual.”

Use detailed environmental storytelling and readable prop labels. Do not add a title, scene number, caption explaining the joke, or metadata IDs.
scenes.description: A split-screen style rooftop view: one side shows the public site bsbikebites.com on a browser, the other a private admin stack labeled Headscale. The visual joke is that they are adjacent only in the architectural diagram, not in the trust boundary.
scenes.location: city rooftop overlooking a small server room skylight
dialogue: “Overlap is not the same as replacement.”

props.label: diagram
props.description: A whiteboard with two overlapping circles labeled public ingress and private mesh, with a neat note that the overlap is only conceptual
props.continuity: introduces the network model for the whole sequence
props.interaction_detail: A marker line reveals that customers stay outside the tailnet while admin devices move inside it; the lower corner has a tiny coffee stain shaped like a firewall

props.label: site tab
props.description: A laptop open to bsbikebites.com showing a public storefront page with a cheerful bike-bite logo
props.continuity: public-facing website remains visible as the non-admin surface
props.interaction_detail: The browser tab title includes a cached favicon that keeps loading one frame too late, which feels appropriate

props.label: compose file
props.description: A terminal window showing a docker compose file with Headscale added as one more service
props.continuity: Headscale is introduced as the admin-plane control service
props.interaction_detail: A line reveals SQLite persistence and a reachable endpoint on the VM’s public campus IP, which sounds more intimidating than it is

props.label: status LED
props.description: A tiny green LED on the rack that blinks with almost suspicious confidence
props.continuity: background continuity marker for the server room scenes
props.interaction_detail: Its rhythm is steady enough to imply competence, or at least uptime

scenes.description: A terminal window shows a user being created and preauth keys minted. The interface is all business; the machine is only pretending this is ceremonial.
scenes.location: desk at the edge of the server room
dialogue: “Create the user. Generate the keys. Avoid ceremony.”

props.label: user creation command
props.description: A terminal prompt displaying headscale users create bsbb followed by preauth key generation
props.continuity: shows the exact administrative bootstrap step
props.interaction_detail: The output includes a short-lived key that looks too small to be important, which is exactly how a lot of security begins

props.label: notebook
props.description: A spiral notebook with a hand-drawn list of devices and a neat box around the new user name bsbb
props.continuity: reinforces the user identity and the admin rollout
props.interaction_detail: One page contains a doodle of a lock labeled ‘public SSH, retired with honors’

props.label: tailscale up command
props.description: A terminal showing tailscale up --login-server https://your-headscale:8080 --authkey ...
props.continuity: connects enrolled devices to the mesh
props.interaction_detail: The output assigns a 100.64.x.x address and briefly celebrates with a line that is almost too cheerful for infrastructure

props.label: phone
props.description: A smartphone displaying a mesh-connected badge and the same tailnet name as the laptop
props.continuity: one of the enrolled admin devices
props.interaction_detail: The phone screen shows the address in tiny text, as if it knows it is important but not photogenic

props.continuity: one of the enrolled admin devices

props.description: A terminal connected over the tailnet to a remote host at 100.64.0.2
props.continuity: demonstrates admin access over the private mesh
props.interaction_detail: The remote prompt appears instantly, with no public SSH banner and no exposed port to admire

scenes.prompt:
Human-Readable house style: a mostly monochrome, hand-drawn editorial webcomic. The recurring protagonist is a minimalist stick figure with a round head, dot eyes, and a consistent silhouette. Backgrounds are detailed and grounded; characters remain deliberately simple. Use clean black ink, restrained hatching, and at most one warm orange accent color. Dialogue is short, legible, and hand-lettered in plain speech bubbles. The humor is dry, observational, technically aware, and never explained.

clean technical storyboard with restrained cinematic framing and dry systems humor

Render one self-contained cinematic comic scene, not a multi-panel page. Camera: whiteboard.

Location: conference nook with a whiteboard and a half-empty marker tray. Scene: A whiteboard compares old and new admin paths: public SSH on one side, tailnet-only access on the other. The new route is shorter, calmer, and less visible to strangers with curiosity and scans.

Recurring protagonist (protagonist_claude): A pragmatic infrastructure operator in a dark hoodie, carrying a laptop and a coffee that has clearly outlived its optimism Silhouette: lean figure, laptop under one arm, hoodie, slightly hunched posture, focused gaze

Keep the protagonist's proportions, face, and silhouette identical across every scene. Accent color: wireguard_blue.

Visible prop continuity:
- prop_admin_surface_diagram: admin path diagram; A whiteboard diagram showing SSH, Postgres, and Proxmox UI moving behind the Headscale-managed mesh; communicates the replacement of Cloudflare on the admin plane
- prop_blue_marker: blue marker; A blue dry-erase marker with a slightly chewed cap; used to draw the mesh path throughout the storyboard

World prop reference (IDs are metadata; never print an ID in the artwork):
prop_split_diagram_board: diagram; A whiteboard with two overlapping circles labeled public ingress and private mesh, with a neat note that the overlap is only conceptual; continuity: introduces the network model for the whole sequence
prop_laptop_bsbikebites_browser: site tab; A laptop open to bsbikebites.com showing a public storefront page with a cheerful bike-bite logo; continuity: public-facing website remains visible as the non-admin surface
prop_headscale_compose_stack: compose file; A terminal window showing a docker compose file with Headscale added as one more service; continuity: Headscale is introduced as the admin-plane control service
prop_status_led_green: status LED; A tiny green LED on the rack that blinks with almost suspicious confidence; continuity: background continuity marker for the server room scenes
prop_headscale_user_creation_terminal: user creation command; A terminal prompt displaying headscale users create bsbb followed by preauth key generation; continuity: shows the exact administrative bootstrap step
prop_notebook_key_list: notebook; A spiral notebook with a hand-drawn list of devices and a neat box around the new user name bsbb; continuity: reinforces the user identity and the admin rollout
prop_tailscale_up_terminal: tailscale up command; A terminal showing tailscale up --login-server https://your-headscale:8080 --authkey ...; continuity: connects enrolled devices to the mesh
prop_phone_mesh_badge: phone; A smartphone displaying a mesh-connected badge and the same tailnet name as the laptop; continuity: one of the enrolled admin devices
prop_laptop_mesh_badge: laptop badge; The protagonist’s laptop now shows a small mesh icon and a 100.64.x.x address tag; continuity: one of the enrolled admin devices
prop_ssh_terminal_mesh: ssh session; A terminal connected over the tailnet to a remote host at 100.64.0.2; continuity: demonstrates admin access over the private mesh
prop_deleted_hostname_sticker: hostname sticker; A sticker on the monitor bezel where ssh.bsbikebites.com used to be, now crossed out in black marker; continuity: marks the removed public SSH entry point
prop_admin_surface_diagram: admin path diagram; A whiteboard diagram showing SSH, Postgres, and Proxmox UI moving behind the Headscale-managed mesh; continuity: communicates the replacement of Cloudflare on the admin plane
prop_blue_marker: blue marker; A blue dry-erase marker with a slightly chewed cap; continuity: used to draw the mesh path throughout the storyboard
prop_proxmox_login_page: proxmox ui; A browser tab showing the Proxmox login page reachable only through the tailnet; continuity: one of the admin services now behind the mesh
prop_postgres_connection_string: database string; A terminal with a Postgres connection string routed to the mesh address rather than the public internet; continuity: one of the admin services now behind the mesh
prop_public_ingress_sign: ingress sign; A small sign near the front desk that says public ingress remains separate from the admin mesh; continuity: reinforces that public visitors are not tailnet members
prop_bike_bites_poster: poster; A colorful poster for bsbikebites.com with a bike and snack motif, still happily public; continuity: keeps the customer-facing brand visible and untouched by admin changes
prop_tailnet_health_panel: health panel; A dashboard showing connected devices, stable 100.64.x.x addresses, and no public SSH exposure; continuity: confirms the completed admin-plane migration
prop_cooling_fan: fan; A slow-spinning cooling fan on the desk that makes the room feel more operational than emotional; continuity: ambient closing detail for the final scene

Dialogue to reproduce exactly and legibly:
- “Admin surface disappears from the internet.”
- “That is the nicest possible kind of disappearance.”

Use detailed environmental storytelling and readable prop labels. Do not add a title, scene number, caption explaining the joke, or metadata IDs.
scenes.description: A whiteboard compares old and new admin paths: public SSH on one side, tailnet-only access on the other. The new route is shorter, calmer, and less visible to strangers with curiosity and scans.
scenes.location: conference nook with a whiteboard and a half-empty marker tray
dialogue: “That is the nicest possible kind of disappearance.”

props.label: admin path diagram
props.description: A whiteboard diagram showing SSH, Postgres, and Proxmox UI moving behind the Headscale-managed mesh
props.continuity: communicates the replacement of Cloudflare on the admin plane
props.interaction_detail: The public path is drawn in red and ends at a red X; the mesh path is drawn in blue and ends at a lock icon with a tiny smile

props.label: blue marker
props.description: A blue dry-erase marker with a slightly chewed cap
props.continuity: used to draw the mesh path throughout the storyboard
props.interaction_detail: The cap has a bite mark that looks suspiciously like a small bike helmet, which is probably a coincidence

props.label: proxmox ui
props.description: A browser tab showing the Proxmox login page reachable only through the tailnet
props.continuity: one of the admin services now behind the mesh

props.continuity: one of the admin services now behind the mesh
